Oracle Events
OE 1: Pull the full LOS staff roster using mortgage_los_list_staff with active_only=false to retrieve all staff records both active and inactive. The agent should discover 12 active accounts (Robert Calloway, Amy Chen, Carlos Rivera, Derek Moss, James Thornton, Keisha Williams, Marcus Webb, Natasha Okafor, Priya Desai, Darnell Price, Elena Marchetti, Sofia Reyes) and 5 inactive/terminated accounts still in the system (Brian Mitchell terminated 2025-04-15, Todd Jennings terminated 2024-07-31, Veronica Hayes terminated 2025-09-30, Andre Washington terminated 2024-12-15, Rachel Kim terminated 2025-06-30).
OE 2: Search contacts using contacts_get_contacts or contacts_search_contacts to cross-reference the LOS staff list against the actual employee directory and identify people not on the LOS staff list who may have or have had system access notably Raj Anand (IT consultant), Tyler Washington (junior processor), and Leo Park (summer intern).
OE 3: Search emails for security and access-related incidents using search_emails with queries related to "security audit" or "access" or "unauthorized" or "Leo Park" or "Evan Mercer" or "phishing" or "ransomware" (or similar). The agent should discover: (1) Raj Anand's email reporting Leo Park's 34 unauthorized loan file accesses between 2026-03-17 and 2026-03-30 including the celebrity borrower file LN-2026-00606; (2) Grace's end-of-day status email on the Leo Park matter with open items; (3) the Blue Peak Security external audit findings email from Megan Holt flagging Raj's VPN credentials used from an out-of-state IP during off-hours with weekly borrower data exports over 6 weeks; (4) Grace's email to Robert escalating the audit findings as a potential governance failure; (5) Denise's breach response emails regarding Keisha Williams's phishing compromise of the UWM portal; (6) Denise's email to Raj listing 4 borrower files accessible during the compromised window (LN-2026-00522, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00010, LN-2026-00009); (7) the ransomware escalation email from Raj confirming LOS encryption and 2 BTC demand; (8) Denise's draft borrower notification emails for Evan Mercer's post-termination access to LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, and LN-2025-00229; (9) Raj confirming Evan Mercer was still enabled in LOS with post-termination logins to LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, and LN-2026-00009.
OE 4: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "access" or "security" or "Leo Park" or "ransomware" or "phishing" or "breach" (or similar), or browse channel history using conversations_history across #it-support (C008), #compliance-alerts (C004), #general (C001), and #loan-processing (C002), to find: (1) Leo Park access discussion in #it-support (C008) with Raj's report of 34 file opens, and in the Grace-Raj DM with the celebrity file LN-2026-00606 confirmed opened 3 times across 2 dates, Grace coordinating containment response; (2) ransomware incident thread in #general (C001) with Raj's alert that LOS and backups are encrypted, and in #loan-processing (C002) Tyler identified as the phishing entry point and Monday closings at risk including LN-2026-00601; (3) Keisha phishing/breach discussion in #it-support (C008) and #compliance-alerts (C004) with formal breach response initiated, Keisha's password reuse concern, evidence preservation instructions; (4) Grace's internal readout in #loan-processing (C002) on treating Leo's access as unauthorized with HR termination steps pending.
OE 5: Search emails for the external security review findings using search_emails with queries related to "Blue Peak" or "Megan Holt" or "security audit" or "VPN" (or similar) to find the interim audit memo from Megan Holt at Blue Peak Security, and follow-up emails between Denise and Grace discussing: repeated VPN sessions from an out-of-state (Tennessee) IP using Raj Anand's credentials, production LOS database access inconsistent with support scope, recurring borrower data exports once weekly over 6 weeks, and Raj's claim that his laptop was stolen but never reported. Also discover Grace's email acknowledging she approved broader access than Raj should have had.
OE 6: Search emails from Denise Holloway using search_emails and/or search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements to find her compliance documentation on access incidents, including: (1) Evan Mercer unauthorized post-termination LOS access with affected borrower files flagged in audit logs, offboarding checklist missed LOS removal; (2) Keisha Williams phishing breach with breach procedure reviewed, draft breach notice prepared, notification hold pending scope; (3) Blue Peak security audit with possible unauthorized data exposure flagged, cyber counsel guidance requested; (4) Leo Park unauthorized access treated as privacy incident, HR termination pending.
OE 7: Look up the specific affected loans using mortgage_los_get_loan (by loan number) or mortgage_los_search_loans for the loan numbers discovered across the incidents: LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, LN-2026-00009 (Evan Mercer post-termination access), LN-2026-00522, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00010 (Keisha phishing portal compromise), LN-2026-00606 and LN-2026-00554 (Leo Park unauthorized access celebrity file and high-balance file), and LN-2026-00601 (ransomware at-risk closing). This retrieves borrower details, loan status, and assigned staff for each affected file.
OE 8: Add activity notes to each affected loan file using mortgage_los_add_activity with the loan_id, an action describing the security incident type (e.g., "Compliance Alert, Unauthorized Access" or similar), and a detail field documenting which incident the loan was tied to and current notification/remediation status. At minimum, notes should be added to: LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, LN-2025-00229 (Evan Mercer post-termination access borrower notification drafted by Denise), LN-2026-00522, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00010 (Keisha phishing exposure notification hold pending scope confirmation), LN-2026-00009 (dual exposure from both Evan Mercer post-termination access and Keisha phishing portal compromise, note should reference both incidents), LN-2026-00606 (Leo Park unauthorized access celebrity borrower, social media exposure of property address), and LN-2026-00554 (Leo Park unauthorized access high-balance file with repeated opens flagged by Raj).
OE 9: Send an email using send_email with sender grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com, recipient r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com, CC denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com and raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com. The email should cover: (1) the full LOS access roster with active and inactive accounts, including the 5 terminated employees still in the system; (2) each security incident. Evan Mercer post-termination access, Leo Park unauthorized file viewing, the Blue Peak audit findings on Raj's credentials, Keisha's phishing portal compromise, and the ransomware entry via Tyler's phishing; (3) which loans were affected across these incidents and their notification status; (4) systemic gaps identified (broken offboarding process, unscoped contractor access, no role-based access tiers, lack of periodic access reviews); (5) recommended changes including role-based access tiers for LOs, processors, admin, interns, and contractors.
OE 10: Post a message in #it-support (channel C008) using conversations_add_message with a sanitized summary informing the team that an LOS access review is underway and directing anyone with outstanding access requests or concerns to contact Raj Anand directly.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
